operations.value: Laboratory digestion models suggest magnesium bisglycinate can release a relatively high proportion of available magnesium, but in-vitro bioavailability findings do not prove superior absorption or better health outcomes in people.
operations.notes: Separates formulation chemistry from clinical efficacy and avoids overstating an in-vitro comparison.

sources.title: In Vitro Evaluation of Bioavailability of Mg from Daily Food Rations, Dietary Supplements and Medicinal Products from the Polish Market

notes: Adds a qualified bioavailability claim without equating laboratory release with clinical benefit.
